posts.title: The Process Is the Punishment
posts.subtitle: Pxie filed fourteen months ago. The plaintiff now wants more time. In a civil case like this one, the trial is the least important thing that happens.

posts.title: The Defendant Is in Miami. The Harm Came From Oxford.
posts.subtitle: Civil liability in Doe v. Bonnell will land on Destiny. The catfisher who dropped the videos is in the UK on a burner account. The system is working.

posts.title: The Cypherpunk Who Filed an S-1
posts.subtitle: Adam Back is taking a Bitcoin company public. If he's Satoshi, the SEC's disclosure rules are about to answer the question seventeen years of speculation couldn't.

posts.title: The Bluff Is Over. The Price Isn't.
posts.subtitle: Five independent forces are holding DDR5 at $400 — and not one of them is the demand that drove it there in the first place.

posts.title: The $71 Billion Bluff
posts.subtitle: OpenAI's letter of intent was non-binding. The market panic it triggered wasn't. Inside the deal that broke DRAM pricing for three years.
